trackingsummarydata.subject: This week in A&S: Meet new Klarman fellows, visiting journalists
trackingsummarydata.title: FSSG - 02/09/26

urls.page_title: College of Arts and Sciences announces 2026 Klarman Fellows
urls.page_summary: The 12 early-career scholars will pursue research in the sciences, social sciences and humanities.
urls.page_title: Three Cornellians named Schwarzman Scholars for study in China | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: A Cornell student and two alumni have been named Schwarzman Scholars for the 2026-27 academic year and will spend it in a master’s program in global affairs at Beijing’s Tsinghua University.
urls.page_title: Weiss and Provost awards honor outstanding faculty | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Seven teaching faculty from across the university have been awarded Cornell’s highest honors for graduate and undergraduate teaching.
urls.page_title: Three Distinguished Visiting Journalists head to campus
urls.page_summary: New York Times columnist Bret Stephens, New York Times White House correspondent Zolan Kanno-Youngs and ProPublica investigative reporter Keri Blakinger ’14 will visit Cornell this spring.
urls.page_title: Art offers access to true self
urls.page_summary: Psychology researcher Jordan Wylie and colleagues found that artistic excellence, rather than moral excellence, offers greater access to one’s true self.
urls.page_title: Cornell in Los Angeles: Info Sessions - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Cornell in Los Angeles is a new study away semester open to all Cornell undergraduate students! Spend the Spring 2027 semester in the global entertainment capital, earning Cornell class credits and having signature L.A. experiences offered by the ...
urls.page_title: Four on faculty to receive DOE early-career grants | Cornell Chronicle
urls.page_summary: Four Cornell faculty members are among 99 researchers across the U.S. who have been awarded grants by the U.S. Department of Energy as part of its Office of Science Early Career Research Program.
urls.page_title: Humanities Scholars Program Winter Open House - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Cornell first-years and sophomores from ANY college across campus: considering a major or minor in a humanities field? Interested in completing a humanities research project on a topic of your choosing? If so, come to the Humanities Scholars Progr...
urls.page_title: Composer Michael Abels, famous for work on Jordan Peele films, to visit March 6-7
urls.page_summary: Events include film screenings, panel discussions and a concert by the Barbara & Richard T. Silver Wind Symphony.
urls.page_title: Makiko Nishikaze: Chamber Works (CU Music) - Cornell
urls.page_summary: Join us for the final evening recital of guest composer Makiko Nishikaze’s visit to Cornell. A wide array of chamber works from throughout Nishikaze’s eclectic career will be performed by members of the Cornell community, as well as by the compose...
